analysis.reasoning: Core message: The tweet states the EU proudly supports UN Women, claims this support has made a real difference for women and girls, and urges not to backtrack on their rights during COVID-19. Evaluation: EU1 (successful story) – Implicitly positive but not about history or overcoming internal obstacles; not promoted. EU2 (united) – No mention of intra-EU unity or agreements; not promoted. EU3 (useful for its citizens) – Focus is on women and girls broadly via UN Women and COVID-19, not specifically on EU-internal citizen benefits; not promoted. EU4 (avant-garde) – No pioneering regulation or leadership framing; not promoted. EU5 (global champion of fair causes) – Yes: it highlights EU leadership/support for gender rights and protection of women and girls’ rights, a fair cause globally. EU6 (contributes to a better world) – Yes: showcases EU’s contribution to global well-being through support to UN Women and safeguarding rights amid COVID-19, indicating multilateral cooperation and positive global impact. Final determination: EU5 and EU6.
